title: syringa reticulata subsp. pekinensis
intro: Syringa reticulata subsp. pekinensis is an ornamental flowering shrub native to East Asia, specifically China, and is a subspecies of the Chinese lilac tree. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Syringa reticulata subsp. pekinensis include reducing fever, alleviating coughs and sore throats, and treating respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used to make medicinal wines and tonics, although its use is largely regional and not widely documented.
